# @d3plus/core

[![NPM version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@d3plus/core.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@d3plus/core)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/d3plus/d3plus/graph/badge.svg?flag=core)](https://codecov.io/gh/d3plus/d3plus/flags)

Data visualization made easy. A javascript library that extends the popular D3.js to enable fast and beautiful visualizations.

## Installing

If using npm, `npm install @d3plus/core`. Otherwise, you can download the [latest release from GitHub](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/releases/latest) or load from a [CDN](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@d3plus/core).

```js
import {*} from "@d3plus/core";
```

In a vanilla environment, a `d3plus` global is exported from the pre-bundled version:

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@d3plus/core"></script>
<script>
  console.log(d3plus);
</script>
```

## Examples

Live examples can be found on [d3plus.org](https://d3plus.org/), which includes a collection of example visualizations using [@d3plus/react](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/tree/main/packages/react).

## API Reference

| Classes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`Area`](#area) | Creates SVG areas based on an array of data. |
| [`AreaPlot`](#areaplot) | Creates an area plot based on an array of data. |
| [`Axis`](#axis) | Creates an SVG scale based on an array of data. |
| [`AxisBottom`](#axisbottom) | Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn below the horizontal domain path. Extends all functional |
| [`AxisLeft`](#axisleft) | Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn to the left of the vertical domain path. Extends all fun |
| [`AxisRight`](#axisright) | Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn to the right of the vertical domain path. Extends all fu |
| [`AxisTop`](#axistop) | Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn above the vertical domain path. Extends all functionalit |
| [`Bar`](#bar) | Creates SVG areas based on an array of data. |
| [`BarChart`](#barchart) | Creates a bar chart based on an array of data. |
| [`BaseClass`](#baseclass) | Provides shared configuration, event handling, and locale management inherited by all d3plus classes. |
| [`Box`](#box) | Creates SVG box based on an array of data. |
| [`BoxWhisker`](#boxwhisker) | Creates a simple box and whisker based on an array of data. |
| [`BumpChart`](#bumpchart) | Creates a bump chart based on an array of data. |
| [`Circle`](#circle) | Creates SVG circles based on an array of data. |
| [`ColorScale`](#colorscale) | Creates an SVG color scale based on an array of data. |
| [`Donut`](#donut) | Extends the Pie visualization to create a donut chart. |
| [`Geomap`](#geomap) | Creates a geographical map with zooming, panning, image tiles, and the ability to layer choropleth paths and coordinate  |
| [`Image`](#image) | Creates SVG images based on an array of data. |
| [`Legend`](#legend) | Creates an SVG legend based on an array of data. |
| [`Line`](#line) | Creates SVG lines based on an array of data. |
| [`LinePlot`](#lineplot) | Creates a line plot based on an array of data. |
| [`Matrix`](#matrix) | Creates a simple rows/columns Matrix view of any dataset. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-matrix/g |
| [`Network`](#network) | Creates a network visualization based on a defined set of nodes and edges. [Click here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plu |
| [`Pack`](#pack) | Uses the [d3 pack layout](https://github.com/d3/d3-hierarchy#pack) to creates Circle Packing chart based on an array of  |
| [`Path`](#path) | Creates SVG Paths based on an array of data. |
| [`Pie`](#pie) | Uses the [d3 pie layout](https://github.com/d3/d3-shape#pies) to creates SVG arcs based on an array of data. |
| [`Plot`](#plot) | Creates an x/y plot based on an array of data. |
| [`Priestley`](#priestley) | Creates a priestley timeline based on an array of data. |
| [`Radar`](#radar) | Creates a radar visualization based on an array of data. |
| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix) | Creates a radial layout of a rows/columns Matrix of any dataset. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-m |
| [`Rect`](#rect) | Creates SVG rectangles based on an array of data. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-shape/getting-st |
| [`Rings`](#rings) | Creates a ring visualization based on a defined set of nodes and edges. [Click here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-n |
| [`Sankey`](#sankey) | Creates a sankey visualization based on a defined set of nodes and links. [Click here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus |
| [`Shape`](#shape) | An abstracted class for generating shapes. |
| [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea) | Creates a stacked area plot based on an array of data. |
| [`TextBox`](#textbox) | Creates a wrapped text box for each point in an array of data. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-tex |
| [`Timeline`](#timeline) | Creates an interactive timeline brush component for selecting time periods within a visualization. |
| [`Tooltip`](#tooltip) | Creates HTML tooltips in the body of a webpage. |
| [`Tree`](#tree) | Uses d3's [tree layout](https://github.com/d3/d3-hierarchy#tree) to create a tidy tree chart based on an array of data. |
| [`Treemap`](#treemap) | Uses the [d3 treemap layout](https://github.com/mbostock/d3/wiki/Treemap-Layout) to creates SVG rectangles based on an a |
| [`Viz`](#viz) | Creates an x/y plot based on an array of data. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-treemap/getting-sta |
| [`Whisker`](#whisker) | Creates SVG whisker based on an array of data. |

| Functions | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`accessor`](#accessor) | Wraps an object key in a simple accessor function. |
| [`configPrep`](#configprep) | Preps a config object for d3plus data, and optionally bubbles up a specific nested type. When using this function, you m |
| [`constant`](#constant) | Wraps non-function variables in a simple return function. |

| Variables | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`RESET`](#reset) | String constant used to reset an individual config property. |

| Interfaces | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`AxisConfig`](#axisconfig) |  |
| [`D3plusConfig`](#d3plusconfig) |  |
| [`TooltipConfig`](#tooltipconfig) |  |

<a id="hitarea"></a>

##### hitArea()

###### Call Signature

> **hitArea**(): ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) \| `null`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1180](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1180)

The mouse hit area accessor function.

###### Returns

((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) \| `null`

###### Example

```ts
function(d, i, shape) {
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`hitArea`](#hitarea-6)

##### labelBounds()

###### Call Signature

> **labelBounds**(): ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1251](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1251)

The label bounds accessor function.

###### Returns

((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

###### Example

```ts
function(d, i, shape) {
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`labelBounds`](#labelbounds-6)

<a id="locale"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

<a id="on"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

<a id="translate"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:215](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L215)

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |

<a id="areaplot"></a>

### AreaPlot

Defined in: [charts/AreaPlot.ts:13](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/AreaPlot.ts#L13)

Creates an area plot based on an array of data.

#### Example

```ts
new d3plus.Plot()
.baseline(0)
.discrete("x")
.shape("Area")
```

#### Extends

- [`Plot`](#plot)

#### Extended by

- [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea)

#### Indexable

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

#### Methods

<a id="active-1"></a>

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:967](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L967)

The active callback function for highlighting shapes.

| `_?` | `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `boolean`) |

##### aggs()

<a id="annotations"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`annotations`](#annotations-5)

<a id="ariahidden"></a>

##### ariaHidden()

> **ariaHidden**(`_?`: `boolean`): `boolean` \| [`AreaPlot`](#areaplot)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:992](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L992)

The "aria-hidden" attribute of the containing SVG element. The default value is "false", but if you need to hide the SVG from screen readers set this property to "true".

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| [`AreaPlot`](#areaplot)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`ariaHidden`](#ariahidden-11)

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. The value provided can either be `true` or `false` to toggle the behavior for all shape types, or a keyed Object for each shape type (ie. `{Bar: false, Circle: true, Line: false}`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
var data = {id: "alpha", value: 10, lci: 9, hci: 11};
...
// Accessor functions
.confidence([function(d) { return d.lci }, function(d) { return d.hci }])

// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

<a id="data-1"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): [`AreaPlot`](#areaplot) \| `DataPoint`[]

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

[`AreaPlot`](#areaplot) \| `DataPoint`[]

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`data`](#data-25)

Defines the mapping between data and shape. The value can be a String matching a key in each data point (default is "id"), or an accessor Function that returns a unique value for each data point. Additionally, an Array of these values may be provided if the visualization supports nested hierarchies.

<a id="labelposition"></a>

##### labelPosition()

> **labelPosition**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition-5)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

A JavaScript [sort comparator function](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/sort) that receives each shape Class (ie. "Circle", "Line", etc) as it's comparator arguments. Shapes are drawn in groups based on their type, so you are defining the layering order for all shapes of said type.

<a id="locale-1"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-1"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="timelocale"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-1"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:215](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L215)

<a id="axisbottom"></a>

### AxisBottom

Defined in: [components/AxisBottom.ts:6](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/AxisBottom.ts#L6)

<a id="locale-2"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-2"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="timelocale-1"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-2"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`translate`](#translate-1)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. ###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |

###### Example

<a id="axisleft"></a>

### AxisLeft

Defined in: [components/AxisLeft.ts:6](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/AxisLeft.ts#L6)

Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn to the left of the vertical domain path. Extends all functionality of the base [Axis](#Axis) class.

#### Extends

- [`Axis`](#axis)

<a id="locale-3"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-3"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`on`](#on-1)

<a id="timelocale-2"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-3"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`translate`](#translate-1)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |

###### Example

<a id="axisright"></a>

### AxisRight

Defined in: [components/AxisRight.ts:6](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/AxisRight.ts#L6)

Shorthand method for creating an axis where the ticks are drawn to the right of the vertical domain path. Extends all functionality of the base [Axis](#Axis) class.

- [`Axis`](#axis)

<a id="locale-4"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

<a id="on-4"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="timelocale-3"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-4"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`translate`](#translate-1)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string` |

###### Returns

###### Example

<a id="axistop"></a>

### AxisTop

<a id="locale-5"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

```ts
})
```

<a id="timelocale-4"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-5"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`translate`](#translate-1)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string` |

###### Returns

###### Example

```ts
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

##### labelBounds()

###### Call Signature

> **labelBounds**(): ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1251](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1251)

The label bounds accessor function.

###### Returns

((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

###### Example

```ts
function(d, i, shape) {
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

###### Inherited from

<a id="locale-6"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture-1"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

<a id="translate-6"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

<a id="barchart"></a>

### BarChart

Defined in: [charts/BarChart.ts:13](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/BarChart.ts#L13)

Creates a bar chart based on an array of data.

#### Example

```ts
new d3plus.Plot()
.baseline(0)
.discrete("x")
.shape("Bar")
```

#### Extends

- [`Plot`](#plot)

#### Indexable

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

<a id="annotations-1"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`annotations`](#annotations-5)

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. <a id="data-8"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`BarChart`](#barchart)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`BarChart`](#barchart)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`data`](#data-25)

<a id="labelposition-1"></a>

##### labelPosition()

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition-5)

<a id="totalpadding-1"></a>

##### totalPadding()

> **totalPadding**(`_?`: `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`)): `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`BarChart`](#barchart)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1841](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1841)

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`BarChart`](#barchart)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`totalPadding`](#totalpadding-11)

A pass-through to the underlying [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) config used for the y-axis. Includes additional functionality where passing "auto" as the value for the [scale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis.scale) method will determine if the scale should be "linear" or "log" based on the provided data.

*Note:* If a "domain" array is passed to the y-axis config, it will be reversed.

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

<a id="locale-7"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-7"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="translate-7"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:215](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L215)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts

return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

<a id="translate-8"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

##### whiskerMode()

###### Call Signature

> **whiskerMode**(): (`string` \| `number`)[]

Defined in: [shapes/Box.ts:449](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Box.ts#L449)

Determines the value used for each whisker. Can be passed a single value to apply for both whiskers, or an Array of 2 values for the lower and upper whiskers (in that order). Accepted values are `"tukey"`, `"extent"`, or a Number representing a quantile.

###### Returns

(`string` \| `number`)[]

### BoxWhisker

<a id="annotations-2"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. The value provided can either be `true` or `false` to toggle the behavior for all shape types, or a keyed Object for each shape type (ie. `{Bar: false, Circle: true, Line: false}`).

| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

`any`

###### Example

```ts
var data = {id: "alpha", value: 10, lci: 9, hci: 11};
...
// Accessor functions
.confidence([function(d) { return d.lci }, function(d) { return d.hci }])

// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

<a id="data-10"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`BoxWhisker`](#boxwhisker)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`BoxWhisker`](#boxwhisker)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`data`](#data-25)

<a id="labelposition-2"></a>

##### labelPosition()

> **labelPosition**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition-5)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

> **shape**(`_?`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`)): `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) \| [`BoxWhisker`](#boxwhisker)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1552](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1552)

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) |

###### Returns

`string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) \| [`BoxWhisker`](#boxwhisker)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`shape`](#shape-17)

<a id="shapesort-2"></a>

##### shapeSort()

> **shapeSort**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2162](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2162)

A JavaScript [sort comparator function](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/sort) that receives each shape Class (ie. "Circle", "Line", etc) as it's comparator arguments. Shapes are drawn in groups based on their type, so you are defining the layering order for all shapes of said type.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`shapeSort`](#shapesort-5)

Tells the subtitle whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the subtitle appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="bumpchart"></a>

### BumpChart

Defined in: [charts/BumpChart.ts:33](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/BumpChart.ts#L33)

Creates a bump chart based on an array of data.

#### Example

```ts
new d3plus.Plot()
.discrete("x")
.shape("Line")
.y2(d => this._y(d))
.yConfig({
tickFormat: val => {
  const data = this._formattedData;
  const xDomain = this._xDomain;
  const startData = data.filter(d => d.x === xDomain[0]);
  const d = startData.find(d => d.y === val);
  return this._drawLabel(d, d.i);
 }
})
.y2Config({
tickFormat: val => {
  const data = this._formattedData;
  const xDomain = this._xDomain;
  const endData = data.filter(d => d.x === xDomain[xDomain.length - 1]);
  const d = endData.find(d => d.y === val);
  return this._drawLabel(d, d.i);
 }
})
.ySort((a, b) => b.y - a.y)
.y2Sort((a, b) => b.y - a.y)
```

#### Extends

- [`Plot`](#plot)

#### Indexable

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

#### Methods

<a id="active-6"></a>

##### active()

> **active**(`_?`: `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `boolean`)): `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `boolean`) \| [`BumpChart`](#bumpchart)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:967](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L967)

###### Parameters

<a id="annotations-3"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`annotations`](#annotations-5)

```ts
// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

<a id="data-11"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`BumpChart`](#bumpchart)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`BumpChart`](#bumpchart)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`data`](#data-25)

<a id="labelposition-3"></a>

##### labelPosition()

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition-5)

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

| ------ | ------ |

<a id="totalpadding-3"></a>

##### totalPadding()

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

##### hitArea()

###### Call Signature

> **hitArea**(): ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) \| `null`

The mouse hit area accessor function.

###### Returns

((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) \| `null`

###### Example

```ts
function(d, i, shape) {
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

<a id="locale-9"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts

        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],

        delimiters: {

          decimal: "."

        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture-2"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

<a id="translate-9"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

### ColorScale
