<a id="axisconfig-1"></a>

##### axisConfig()

###### Call Signature

> **axisConfig**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:791](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L791)

The [ColorScale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale) is constructed by combining an [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) for the ticks/labels and a [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) for the actual color box (or multiple boxes, as in a jenks scale). Because of this, there are separate configs for the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) class used to display the text ([axisConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.axisConfig)) and the [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) class used to draw the color breaks ([rectConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.rectConfig)). This method acts as a pass-through to the config method of the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis). An example usage of this method can be seen [here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-legend/colorScale-dark/).

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>

###### Call Signature

> **axisConfig**(`_`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:792](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L792)

The [ColorScale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale) is constructed by combining an [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) for the ticks/labels and a [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) for the actual color box (or multiple boxes, as in a jenks scale). Because of this, there are separate configs for the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) class used to display the text ([axisConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.axisConfig)) and the [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) class used to draw the color breaks ([rectConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.rectConfig)). This method acts as a pass-through to the config method of the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis). An example usage of this method can be seen [here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-legend/colorScale-dark/).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> |

`this`

<a id="bucketaxis"></a>

##### bucketAxis()

###### Call Signature

> **bucketAxis**(): `boolean`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:820](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L820)

Determines whether or not to use an Axis to display bucket scales (both "buckets" and "jenks"). When set to `false`, bucketed scales will use the `Legend` class to display squares for each range of data. When set to `true`, bucketed scales will be displayed on an `Axis`, similar to "linear" scales.

###### Returns

`boolean`

###### Call Signature

> **bucketAxis**(`_`: `boolean`): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:821](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L821)

Determines whether or not to use an Axis to display bucket scales (both "buckets" and "jenks"). When set to `false`, bucketed scales will use the `Legend` class to display squares for each range of data. When set to `true`, bucketed scales will be displayed on an `Axis`, similar to "linear" scales.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `boolean` |

###### Returns

`this`

<a id="bucketformat"></a>

##### bucketFormat()

###### Call Signature

> **bucketFormat**(): (`tick`: `number`, `i`: `number`, `ticks`: `number`[], `allValues`: `number`[]) => `string`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:831](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L831)

A function for formatting the labels associated to each bucket in a bucket-type scale ("jenks", "quantile", etc). The function is passed four arguments: the start value of the current bucket, it's index in the full Array of buckets, the full Array of buckets, and an Array of every value present in the data used to construct the buckets. Keep in mind that the end value for the bucket is not actually the next bucket in the list, but includes every value up until that next bucket value (less than, but not equal to). By default, d3plus will make the end value slightly less than it's current value, so that it does not overlap with the start label for the next bucket.

###### Returns

(`tick`: `number`, `i`: `number`, `ticks`: `number`[], `allValues`: `number`[]) => `string`

###### Call Signature

> **bucketFormat**(`_`: (`tick`: `number`, `i`: `number`, `ticks`: `number`[], `allValues`: `number`[]) => `string`): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:837](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L837)

A function for formatting the labels associated to each bucket in a bucket-type scale ("jenks", "quantile", etc). The function is passed four arguments: the start value of the current bucket, it's index in the full Array of buckets, the full Array of buckets, and an Array of every value present in the data used to construct the buckets. Keep in mind that the end value for the bucket is not actually the next bucket in the list, but includes every value up until that next bucket value (less than, but not equal to). By default, d3plus will make the end value slightly less than it's current value, so that it does not overlap with the start label for the next bucket.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | (`tick`: `number`, `i`: `number`, `ticks`: `number`[], `allValues`: `number`[]) => `string` |

<a id="buckets"></a>

##### buckets()

###### Call Signature

> **buckets**(): `number` \| `number`[]

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:811](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L811)

The number of discrete buckets to create in a bucketed color scale. Will be overridden by any custom Array of colors passed to the `color` method. Optionally, users can supply an Array of values used to separate buckets, such as `[0, 10, 25, 50, 90]` for a percentage scale. This value would create 4 buckets, with each value representing the break point between each bucket (so 5 values makes 4 buckets).

###### Returns

`number` \| `number`[]

###### Call Signature

> **buckets**(`_`: `number` \| `number`[]): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:812](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L812)

The number of discrete buckets to create in a bucketed color scale. Will be overridden by any custom Array of colors passed to the `color` method. Optionally, users can supply an Array of values used to separate buckets, such as `[0, 10, 25, 50, 90]` for a percentage scale. This value would create 4 buckets, with each value representing the break point between each bucket (so 5 values makes 4 buckets).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `number` \| `number`[] |

`this`

<a id="color-4"></a>

##### color()

###### Call Signature

> **color**(): `string` \| `string`[]

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:881](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L881)

Overrides the default internal logic of `colorMin`, `colorMid`, and `colorMax` to only use just this specified color. If a single color is given as a String, then the scale is interpolated by lightening that color. Otherwise, the function expects an Array of color values to be used in order for the scale.

###### Returns

`string` \| `string`[]

###### Call Signature

> **color**(`_`: `string` \| `string`[]): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:882](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L882)

Overrides the default internal logic of `colorMin`, `colorMid`, and `colorMax` to only use just this specified color. If a single color is given as a String, then the scale is interpolated by lightening that color. Otherwise, the function expects an Array of color values to be used in order for the scale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `string`[] |

###### Returns

`this`

<a id="colormax"></a>

##### colorMax()

###### Call Signature

> **colorMax**(): `string`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:890](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L890)

Defines the color to be used for numbers greater than the value of the `midpoint` on the scale (defaults to `0`). Colors in between this value and the value of `colorMid` will be interpolated, unless a custom Array of colors has been specified using the `color` method.

###### Returns

`string`

<a id="legendconfig-4"></a>

##### legendConfig()

###### Call Signature

> **legendConfig**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:982](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L982)

The [ColorScale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale) is constructed by combining an [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) for the ticks/labels and a [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) for the actual color box (or multiple boxes, as in a jenks scale). Because of this, there are separate configs for the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) class used to display the text ([axisConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.axisConfig)) and the [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) class used to draw the color breaks ([rectConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.rectConfig)). This method acts as a pass-through to the config method of the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis). An example usage of this method can be seen [here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-legend/colorScale-dark/).

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>

###### Call Signature

> **legendConfig**(`_`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>): `this`

Defined in: [components/ColorScale.ts:983](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/ColorScale.ts#L983)

The [ColorScale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale) is constructed by combining an [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) for the ticks/labels and a [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) for the actual color box (or multiple boxes, as in a jenks scale). Because of this, there are separate configs for the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) class used to display the text ([axisConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.axisConfig)) and the [Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect) class used to draw the color breaks ([rectConfig](http://d3plus.org/docs/#ColorScale.rectConfig)). This method acts as a pass-through to the config method of the [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis). An example usage of this method can be seen [here](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-legend/colorScale-dark/).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> |

###### Returns

`this`

<a id="locale-10"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="translate-10"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

<a id="attribution-4"></a>

##### attribution()

> **attribution**(`_?`: `string` \| `boolean`): `string` \| `boolean` \| [`Donut`](#donut)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:999](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L999)

Sets text to be shown positioned absolute on top of the visualization in the bottom-right corner. This is most often used in Geomaps to display the copyright of map tiles. The text is rendered as HTML, so any valid HTML string will render as expected (eg. anchor links work).

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-14"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Donut`](#donut)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Donut`](#donut)

###### Inherited from

[`Pie`](#pie).[`data`](#data-24)

Defines the click functionality of categorical legend squares. When set to false, clicking will hide that category and shift+clicking will solo that category. When set to true, clicking with solo that category and shift+clicking will hide that category.

<a id="legendpadding-4"></a>

##### legendPadding()

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Donut`](#donut)

<a id="geomap"></a>

### Geomap

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:65](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L65)

Creates a geographical map with zooming, panning, image tiles, and the ability to layer choropleth paths and coordinate points. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-geomap/getting-started/) for help getting started.

#### Extends

- [`Viz`](#viz)

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

<a id="data-15"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="fitfilter"></a>

##### fitFilter()

> **fitFilter**(`_?`: `string` \| `string`[] \| ((`d`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `boolean`)): [`Geomap`](#geomap) \| ((`d`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `boolean`)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:570](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L570)

Topojson files sometimes include small geographies that negatively impact how the library determines the default zoom level (for example, a small island or territory far off the coast that is barely visible to the eye). The fitFilter method can be used to remove specific geographies from the logic used to determine the zooming.

The *value* passed can be a single id to remove, an array of ids, or a filter function. Take a look at the [Choropleth Example](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-geomap/getting-started/) to see it in action.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `string`[] \| ((`d`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

[`Geomap`](#geomap) \| ((`d`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `boolean`)

<a id="fitkey"></a>

##### fitKey()

> **fitKey**(`_?`: `string`): `string` \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:591](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L591)

If the topojson being used to determine the zoom fit (either the main [topojson](#Geomap.topojson) object or the [fitObject](#Geomap.fitObject)) contains multiple geographical sets (for example, a file containing state and county boundaries), use this method to indentify which set to use for the zoom fit.

If not specified, the first key in the *Array* returned from using `Object.keys` on the topojson will be used.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` |

###### Returns

`string` \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

<a id="fitobject"></a>

##### fitObject()

> **fitObject**(`_?`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>, `f?`: (`data`: `unknown`) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:607](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L607)

The topojson to be used for the initial projection [fit extent](https://github.com/d3/d3-geo#projection_fitExtent). The value passed should either be a valid Topojson *Object* or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function needs to return the final Topojson *Object*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> | *required* |
| `f?` | (`data`: `unknown`) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> | Topojson data or a URL to load. |

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

<a id="groupby-5"></a>

##### groupBy()

> **groupBy**(`_?`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) \| (`string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`))[]): (`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`[] \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1283](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1283)

Defines the mapping between data and shape. The value can be a String matching a key in each data point (default is "id"), or an accessor Function that returns a unique value for each data point. Additionally, an Array of these values may be provided if the visualization supports nested hierarchies.

###### Parameters

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. <a id="ocean"></a>

##### ocean()

The color visible behind any shapes drawn on the map projection. By default, a color value matching the color used in the map tiles is used to help mask the loading time needed to render the tiles. Any value CSS color value may be used, including hexidecimal, rgb, rgba, and color strings like `"blue"` and `"transparent"`.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` | "#d4dadc"] |

The accessor to be used when detecting coordinate points in the objects passed to the [data](https://d3plus.org/docs/#Viz.data) method. Values are expected to be in the format `[longitude, latitude]`, which is in-line with d3's expected coordinate mapping.

<a id="projection"></a>

##### projection()

> **projection**(`_?`: `string` \| ((...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`)): [`Geomap`](#geomap) \| ((...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:673](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L673)

The map projection used when displaying topojson and coordinate points. All projections from [d3-geo](https://github.com/d3/d3-geo#projections), [d3-geo-projection](https://github.com/d3/d3-geo-projection#api-reference), and [d3-composite-projections](http://geoexamples.com/d3-composite-projections/) are accepted, either as the string name (ie. "geoMercator") or the generator function itself. Map tiles are only usable when the projection is set to Mercator (the default).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`) | "geoMercator" |

[`Geomap`](#geomap) \| ((...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`)

<a id="shape-10"></a>

##### shape()

> **shape**(`_?`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`)): `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1552](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1552)

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

###### Parameters

<a id="tileurl"></a>

##### tileUrl()

> **tileUrl**(`_?`: `string`): `string` \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:731](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L731)

By default, d3plus uses the `light_all` style provided by [CARTO](https://carto.com/location-data-services/basemaps/) for it's map tiles. The [tileUrl](https://d3plus.org/docs/#Geomap.tileUrl) method changes the base URL used for fetching the tiles, as long as the string passed contains `{x}`, `{y}`, and `{z}` variables enclosed in curly brackets for the zoom logic to load the correct tiles.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` | "https://cartodb-basemaps-{s}.global.ssl.fastly.net/light_all/{z}/{x}/{y}.png"] |

###### Returns

`string` \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

<a id="topojson"></a>

##### topojson()

> **topojson**(`_?`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>, `f?`: (`data`: `unknown`) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Geomap.ts:748](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Geomap.ts#L748)

The topojson to be used for drawing geographical paths. The value passed should either be a valid Topojson *Object* or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final Topojson *Obejct*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> | [] |
| `f?` | (`data`: `unknown`) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> | Topojson data or a URL to load. |

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

If the [topojson](#Geomap.topojson) being used contains boundaries that should not be shown, this method can be used to filter them out of the final output. The *value* passed can be a single id to remove, an array of ids, or a filter function.

<a id="topojsonkey"></a>

If the [topojson](#Geomap.topojson) contains multiple geographical sets (for example, a file containing state and county boundaries), use this method to indentify which set to use.

If not specified, the first key in the *Array* returned from using `Object.keys` on the topojson will be used.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` |

###### Returns

`string` \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

<a id="totalpadding-5"></a>

##### totalPadding()

> **totalPadding**(`_?`: `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`)): `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1841](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1841)

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Geomap`](#geomap)

<a id="image"></a>

### Image

Defined in: [shapes/Image.ts:22](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Image.ts#L22)

Creates SVG images based on an array of data.

#### Examples

```ts
var data = {"url": "file.png", "width": "100", "height": "50"};
```

```ts
new Image().data([data]).render();
```

```ts
<image class="d3plus-Image" opacity="1" href="file.png" width="100" height="50" x="0" y="0"></image>
```

```ts
image().data([data])();
```

```ts
image().data([data])(function() { alert("draw complete!"); })
```

#### Methods

<a id="data-16"></a>

<a id="locale-11"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

<a id="on-11"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="translate-11"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

<a id="locale-12"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

```ts
})
```

<a id="texture-3"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn` |

<a id="translate-12"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

<a id="lineplot"></a>

### LinePlot

Defined in: [charts/LinePlot.ts:12](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/LinePlot.ts#L12)

Creates a line plot based on an array of data.

#### Example

```ts
new d3plus.Plot()
.discrete("x")
.shape("Line")
```

#### Extends

<a id="annotations-4"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`annotations`](#annotations-5)

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. The value provided can either be `true` or `false` to toggle the behavior for all shape types, or a keyed Object for each shape type (ie. `{Bar: false, Circle: true, Line: false}`).

| ------ | ------ |

###### Example

```ts
var data = {id: "alpha", value: 10, lci: 9, hci: 11};
...
// Accessor functions
.confidence([function(d) { return d.lci }, function(d) { return d.hci }])

// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

<a id="data-19"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`LinePlot`](#lineplot)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`LinePlot`](#lineplot)

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`data`](#data-25)

Defines the mapping between data and shape. The value can be a String matching a key in each data point (default is "id"), or an accessor Function that returns a unique value for each data point. <a id="labelposition-4"></a>

##### labelPosition()

> **labelPosition**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`Plot`](#plot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition-5)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. ##### shape()

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) |

A JavaScript [sort comparator function](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/sort) that receives each shape Class (ie. "Circle", "Line", etc) as it's comparator arguments. Shapes are drawn in groups based on their type, so you are defining the layering order for all shapes of said type.

| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

`any`

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="matrix"></a>

### Matrix

Defined in: [charts/Matrix.ts:26](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Matrix.ts#L26)

Determines which key in your data should be used for each column in the matrix. Can be either a String that matches a key used in every data point, or an accessor function that receives a data point and it's index in the data array, and is expected to return it's column value.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) |

###### Returns

`string` \| [`Matrix`](#matrix) \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)

###### Example

```ts
function column(d) {
return d.name;
```
```ts
}
```

<a id="data-20"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Matrix`](#matrix)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Matrix`](#matrix)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="row"></a>

##### row()

> **row**(`_?`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)): `string` \| [`Matrix`](#matrix) \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)

Defined in: [charts/Matrix.ts:278](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Matrix.ts#L278)

Determines which key in your data should be used for each row in the matrix. Can be either a String that matches a key used in every data point, or an accessor function that receives a data point and it's index in the data array, and is expected to return it's row value.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) |

###### Returns

`string` \| [`Matrix`](#matrix) \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)

###### Example

```ts
function row(d) {
return d.name;
}
```

<a id="subtitlepadding-7"></a>

##### subtitlePadding()

Tells the subtitle whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the subtitle appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="data-21"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Network`](#network)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Network`](#network)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. <a id="links"></a>

##### links()

> **links**(`_`: `any`, `f?`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Network.ts:586](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Network.ts#L586)

A predefined *Array* of edges that connect each object passed to the [node](#Network.node) method. The `source` and `target` keys in each link need to map to the nodes in one of three ways:
1. The index of the node in the nodes array (as in [this](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-network/getting-started/) example).
2. The actual node *Object* itself.
3. A *String* value matching the `id` of the node.

The value passed should either be an *Array* of data or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. An optional formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final links *Array*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` | - |
| `f?` | `any` | Array of link objects or a URL to load links from. |

###### Returns

`any`

Defines the thickness of the links connecting each node. The value provided can be either a pixel Number to be used for all links, or an accessor function that returns a specific data value to be used in an automatically calculated linear scale.

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `any` |

`any`

<a id="data-22"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Pack`](#pack)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Pack`](#pack)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. ```

Tells the subtitle whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the subtitle appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

```ts
"width": shape.width,

"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

##### labelBounds()

###### Call Signature

> **labelBounds**(): ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1251](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1251)

The label bounds accessor function.

###### Returns

((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`, `aes`: `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\>) => `false` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `null`) \| `null`

###### Example

```ts
function(d, i, shape) {
return {
"width": shape.width,
"height": shape.height,
"x": -shape.width / 2,
"y": -shape.height / 2
};
}
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`labelBounds`](#labelbounds-6)

<a id="locale-13"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

<a id="on-13"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture-4"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

<a id="translate-13"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

<a id="pie"></a>

### Pie

Defined in: [charts/Pie.ts:13](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Pie.ts#L13)

Uses the [d3 pie layout](https://github.com/d3/d3-shape#pies) to creates SVG arcs based on an array of data.

#### Extends

- [`Viz`](#viz)

#### Extended by

- [`Donut`](#donut)

#### Indexable

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

##### ariaHidden()

> **ariaHidden**(`_?`: `boolean`): `boolean` \| [`Pie`](#pie)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:992](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L992)

The "aria-hidden" attribute of the containing SVG element. The default value is "false", but if you need to hide the SVG from screen readers set this property to "true".

<a id="attribution-10"></a>

##### attribution()

> **attribution**(`_?`: `string` \| `boolean`): `string` \| `boolean` \| [`Pie`](#pie)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:999](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L999)

Sets text to be shown positioned absolute on top of the visualization in the bottom-right corner. This is most often used in Geomaps to display the copyright of map tiles. The text is rendered as HTML, so any valid HTML string will render as expected (eg. anchor links work).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `boolean` |

`string` \| `boolean` \| [`Pie`](#pie)

<a id="color-11"></a>

##### color()

> **color**(`_?`: `string` \| `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)): `string` \| `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) \| [`Pie`](#pie)

Defines the main color to be used for each data point in a visualization. Can be either an accessor function or a string key to reference in each data point. If a color value is returned, it will be used as is. If a string is returned, a unique color will be assigned based on the string.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) |

[`Viz`](#viz).[`color`](#color-21)

<a id="colorscalepadding-11"></a>

##### colorScalePadding()

> **colorScalePadding**(`_?`: `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`)): `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Pie`](#pie)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1083](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1083)

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Pie`](#pie)

<a id="colorscaleposition-10"></a>

##### colorScalePosition()

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-24"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Pie`](#pie)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Pie`](#pie)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="annotations-5"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

The "aria-hidden" attribute of the containing SVG element. The default value is "false", but if you need to hide the SVG from screen readers set this property to "true".

Sets text to be shown positioned absolute on top of the visualization in the bottom-right corner. This is most often used in Geomaps to display the copyright of map tiles. The text is rendered as HTML, so any valid HTML string will render as expected (eg. anchor links work).

<a id="buffer-5"></a>

##### buffer()

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2045](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2045)

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. The value provided can either be `true` or `false` to toggle the behavior for all shape types, or a keyed Object for each shape type (ie. `{Bar: false, Circle: true, Line: false}`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="confidence-5"></a>

##### confidence()

The confidence interval as an array of [lower, upper] bounds.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
var data = {id: "alpha", value: 10, lci: 9, hci: 11};
...
// Accessor functions
.confidence([function(d) { return d.lci }, function(d) { return d.hci }])

// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

<a id="data-25"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): [`Plot`](#plot) \| `DataPoint`[]

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

[`Plot`](#plot) \| `DataPoint`[]

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="labelposition-5"></a>

##### labelPosition()

> **labelPosition**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

A pass-through to the underlying [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) config used for the y-axis. Includes additional functionality where passing "auto" as the value for the [scale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis.scale) method will determine if the scale should be "linear" or "log" based on the provided data.

*Note:* If a "domain" array is passed to the y-axis config, it will be reversed.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

When the height of the chart is less than or equal to this pixel value, and the y-axis is not the discrete axis, it will not be shown. This helps produce slick sparklines. Set this value to `0` to disable the behavior entirely.

| ------ | ------ |

`any`

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-26"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Priestley`](#priestley)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Priestley`](#priestley)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

The [paddingInner](https://github.com/d3/d3-scale#band_paddingInner) value of the underlining [Band Scale](https://github.com/d3/d3-scale#band-scales) used to determine the height of each bar. Values should be a ratio between 0 and 1 representing the space in between each rectangle.

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

| ------ | ------ |

Tells the subtitle whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Radar`](#radar)

<a id="colorscaleposition-13"></a>

##### colorScalePosition()

> **colorScalePosition**(`_?`: `string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`)): `string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`) \| [`Radar`](#radar)

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

###### Parameters
