<a id="data-27"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Radar`](#radar)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Radar`](#radar)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines the main color to be used for each data point in a visualization. Can be either an accessor function or a string key to reference in each data point. If a color value is returned, it will be used as is. If a string is returned, a unique color will be assigned based on the string.

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1083](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1083)

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`colorScalePadding`](#colorscalepadding-21)

<a id="colorscaleposition-14"></a>

##### colorScalePosition()

> **colorScalePosition**(`_?`: `string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`)): `string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`) \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1095](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1095)

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`string` \| `boolean` \| (() => `string` \| `boolean`) \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

###### Inherited from

<a id="column-1"></a>

##### column()

> **column**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/RadialMatrix.ts:236](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/RadialMatrix.ts#L236)

Determines which key in your data should be used for each column in the matrix. Can be either a String that matches a key used in every data point, or an accessor function that receives a data point and it's index in the data array, and is expected to return it's column value.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
function column(d) {
return d.name;
}
```

<a id="data-28"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

> **legendPadding**(`_?`: `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`)): `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1434](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1434)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`RadialMatrix`](#radialmatrix)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`legendPadding`](#legendpadding-21)

<a id="row-1"></a>

##### row()

> **row**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/RadialMatrix.ts:292](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/RadialMatrix.ts#L292)

Determines which key in your data should be used for each row in the matrix. Can be either a String that matches a key used in every data point, or an accessor function that receives a data point and it's index in the data array, and is expected to return it's row value.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
function row(d) {
return d.name;
}
```

<a id="rowsort-1"></a>

##### rowSort()

> **rowSort**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/RadialMatrix.ts:313](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/RadialMatrix.ts#L313)

A sort comparator function that is run on the unique set of row values.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
function row(a, b) {
return a.localeCompare(b);
}
```

Tells the timeline whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the timeline appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="locale-14"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`locale`](#locale-15)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

```ts
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {

})

console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture-5"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`texture`](#texture-6)

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

<a id="translate-14"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Shape`](#shape-23).[`translate`](#translate-15)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

### Rings

Defined in: [charts/Rings.ts:15](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Rings.ts#L15)

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-30"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Rings`](#rings)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Rings`](#rings)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="links-1"></a>

##### links()

> **links**(`_`: `any`, `f?`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Rings.ts:601](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Rings.ts#L601)

A predefined *Array* of edges that connect each object passed to the [node](#Rings.node) method. The `source` and `target` keys in each link need to map to the nodes in one of three ways:
1. The index of the node in the nodes array (as in [this](http://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-network/getting-started/) example).
2. The actual node *Object* itself.
3. A *String* value matching the `id` of the node.

The value passed should either be an *Array* of data or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. An optional formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final links *Array*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` | - |
| `f?` | `any` | Array of link objects or a URL to load links from. |

###### Returns

`any`

Defines the thickness of the links connecting each node. The value provided can be either a pixel Number to be used for all links, or an accessor function that returns a specific data value to be used in an automatically calculated linear scale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

`any`

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-31"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Sankey`](#sankey)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Sankey`](#sankey)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. <a id="links-2"></a>

##### links()

> **links**(`_`: `any`, `f?`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Sankey.ts:252](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Sankey.ts#L252)

A predefined *Array* of edges that connect each object passed to the [node](#Sankey.node) method. The `source` and `target` keys in each link need to map to the nodes in one of one way:
1. A *String* value matching the `id` of the node.

The value passed should be an *Array* of data. An optional formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final links *Array*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` | - |
| `f?` | `any` | Array of link objects or a URL to load links from. |

###### Returns

`any`

<a id="nodes-2"></a>

##### nodes()

Defined in: [charts/Sankey.ts:310](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Sankey.ts#L310)

The list of nodes to be used for drawing the network. The value passed must be an *Array* of data.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final node *Array*.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` | - |
| `f?` | `any` | Array of node objects or a URL to load nodes from. |

###### Returns

`any`

<a id="totalpadding-16"></a>

##### totalPadding()

> **totalPadding**(`_?`: `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`)): `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Sankey`](#sankey)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1841](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1841)

Tells the total whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the total appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) |

###### Returns

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Sankey`](#sankey)

<a id="locale-15"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Example

```ts
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="texture-6"></a>

##### texture()

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(): `AccessorFn`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1488](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1488)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Returns

`AccessorFn`

###### Call Signature

> **texture**(`_`: `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn`): `this`

Defined in: [shapes/Shape.ts:1489](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/shapes/Shape.ts#L1489)

Defines the texture used inside of each shape. This uses the [textures.js](https://riccardoscalco.it/textures/) package, and expects either a simple string (`"lines"` or `"circles"`) or a more complex Object containing the various properties of the texture (ie. `{texture: "lines", orientation: "3/8", stroke: "darkorange"}`). If multiple textures are necessary, provide an accsesor Function that returns the correct String/Object for each given data point and index.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `AccessorFn` |

###### Returns

`this`

<a id="translate-15"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

<a id="stackedarea"></a>

### StackedArea

Defined in: [charts/StackedArea.ts:9](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/StackedArea.ts#L9)

Creates a stacked area plot based on an array of data.

#### Example

```ts
new d3plus.AreaPlot()
.stacked(true)
```

#### Extends

- [`AreaPlot`](#areaplot)

#### Indexable

> \[`key`: `string`\]: `any`

#### Methods

<a id="annotations-6"></a>

##### annotations()

> **annotations**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2004](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2004)

Allows drawing custom shapes to be used as annotations in the provided x/y plot. This method accepts custom config objects for the [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) class, either a single config object or an array of config objects. Each config object requires an additional parameter, the "shape", which denotes which [Shape](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Shape) sub-class to use ([Rect](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Rect), [Line](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Line), etc).

Additionally, each config object can also contain an optional "layer" key, which defines whether the annotations will be displayed in "front" or in "back" of the primary visualization shapes. This value defaults to "back" if not present.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`AreaPlot`](#areaplot).[`annotations`](#annotations)

<a id="ariahidden-17"></a>

The "aria-hidden" attribute of the containing SVG element. The default value is "false", but if you need to hide the SVG from screen readers set this property to "true".

###### Parameters

| `_?` | `boolean` |

<a id="attribution-17"></a>

##### attribution()

> **attribution**(`_?`: `string` \| `boolean`): `string` \| `boolean` \| [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:999](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L999)

Sets text to be shown positioned absolute on top of the visualization in the bottom-right corner. This is most often used in Geomaps to display the copyright of map tiles. The text is rendered as HTML, so any valid HTML string will render as expected (eg. anchor links work).

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `boolean` |

<a id="buffer-6"></a>

##### buffer()

> **buffer**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2045](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2045)

Determines whether or not to add additional padding at the ends of x or y scales. The most commone use for this is in Scatter Plots, so that the shapes do not appear directly on the axis itself. The value provided can either be `true` or `false` to toggle the behavior for all shape types, or a keyed Object for each shape type (ie. `{Bar: false, Circle: true, Line: false}`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

<a id="color-18"></a>

##### color()

> **color**(`_?`: `string` \| `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)): `string` \| `false` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`) \| [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea)

Defines the main color to be used for each data point in a visualization. Can be either an accessor function or a string key to reference in each data point. If a color value is returned, it will be used as is. If a string is returned, a unique color will be assigned based on the string.

<a id="colorscalepadding-18"></a>

##### colorScalePadding()

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="confidence-6"></a>

##### confidence()

The confidence interval as an array of [lower, upper] bounds.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
var data = {id: "alpha", value: 10, lci: 9, hci: 11};
...
// Accessor functions
.confidence([function(d) { return d.lci }, function(d) { return d.hci }])

// Or static keys
.confidence(["lci", "hci"])
```

[`AreaPlot`](#areaplot).[`confidence`](#confidence)

<a id="data-33"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`StackedArea`](#stackedarea)

###### Inherited from

[`AreaPlot`](#areaplot).[`data`](#data-1)

<a id="labelposition-6"></a>

##### labelPosition()

> **labelPosition**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Plot.ts:2127](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Plot.ts#L2127)

The behavior to be used when calculating the position and size of each shape's label(s). The value passed can either be the _String_ name of the behavior to be used for all shapes, or an accessor _Function_ that will be provided each data point and will be expected to return the behavior to be used for that data point. The availability and options for this method depend on the default logic for each Shape. As an example, the values "outside" or "inside" can be set for Bar shapes, whose "auto" default will calculate the best position dynamically based on the available space.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Inherited from

[`AreaPlot`](#areaplot).[`labelPosition`](#labelposition)

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. Tells the timeline whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. A pass-through to the underlying [Axis](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis) config used for the y-axis. Includes additional functionality where passing "auto" as the value for the [scale](http://d3plus.org/docs/#Axis.scale) method will determine if the scale should be "linear" or "log" based on the provided data.

*Note:* If a "domain" array is passed to the y-axis config, it will be reversed.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

`any`

<a id="ellipsis"></a>

##### ellipsis()

###### Call Signature

> **ellipsis**(): (`text`: `string`, `line`: `number`) => `string`

Defined in: [components/TextBox.ts:568](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/TextBox.ts#L568)

The function that handles truncated lines. It should return the new value for the line, and is passed 2 arguments: the String of text for the line in question, and the number of the line. By default, an ellipsis is added to the end of any line except if it is the first word that cannot fit (in that case, an empty string is returned).

###### Returns

(`text`: `string`, `line`: `number`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
function(text, line) {
return line ? text.replace(/\.|,$/g, "") + "..." : "";
}
```

###### Call Signature

> **ellipsis**(`_`: `string` \| ((`text`: `string`, `line`: `number`) => `string`)): `this`

Defined in: [components/TextBox.ts:569](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/TextBox.ts#L569)

The function that handles truncated lines. It should return the new value for the line, and is passed 2 arguments: the String of text for the line in question, and the number of the line. By default, an ellipsis is added to the end of any line except if it is the first word that cannot fit (in that case, an empty string is returned).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| ((`text`: `string`, `line`: `number`) => `string`) |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
function(text, line) {
return line ? text.replace(/\.|,$/g, "") + "..." : "";
}
```

<a id="fontfamily-18"></a>

##### fontFamily()

###### Call Signature

> **fontFamily**(): (`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string`

Defined in: [components/TextBox.ts:590](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/TextBox.ts#L590)

Defines the font-family to be used. The value passed can be either a *String* name of a font, a comma-separated list of font-family fallbacks, an *Array* of fallbacks, or a *Function* that returns either a *String* or an *Array*. If supplying multiple fallback fonts, the [fontExists](#fontExists) function will be used to determine the first available font on the client's machine.

###### Returns

(`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string`

###### Call Signature

> **fontFamily**(`_`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string`)): `this`

Defined in: [components/TextBox.ts:591](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/TextBox.ts#L591)

Defines the font-family to be used. The value passed can be either a *String* name of a font, a comma-separated list of font-family fallbacks, an *Array* of fallbacks, or a *Function* that returns either a *String* or an *Array*. If supplying multiple fallback fonts, the [fontExists](#fontExists) function will be used to determine the first available font on the client's machine.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string`) |

###### Returns

`this`

<a id="locale-16"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="translate-16"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. ###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |

<a id="locale-17"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`locale`](#locale-1)

<a id="timelocale-5"></a>

##### timeLocale()

###### Call Signature

> **timeLocale**(): `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

Defined in: [components/Axis.ts:1895](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Axis.ts#L1895)

Defines a custom locale object to be used in time scale. This object must include the following properties: dateTime, date, time, periods, days, shortDays, months, shortMonths. For more information, you can revise [d3p.d3-time-format](https://github.com/d3/d3-time-format/blob/master/README.md#timeFormatLocale).

`Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `undefined`

<a id="translate-17"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`Axis`](#axis).[`translate`](#translate-1)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:215](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L215)

###### Parameters

| ------ | ------ |

###### Example

<a id="tooltip-18"></a>

### Tooltip

Defined in: [components/Tooltip.ts:81](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Tooltip.ts#L81)

Creates HTML tooltips in the body of a webpage.

#### Extends

- [`BaseClass`](#baseclass)

#### Methods

<a id="arrow"></a>

##### arrow()

###### Call Signature

> **arrow**(): (`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`

Defined in: [components/Tooltip.ts:393](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/components/Tooltip.ts#L393)

The inner HTML content of the arrow element, empty by default.

###### Returns

(`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`

###### Example

```ts
function value(d) {
return d.arrow || "";
}
```

###### Call Signature

> **arrow**(`_`: `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i?`: `number`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`)): `this`

The inner HTML content of the arrow element, empty by default.

<a id="locale-18"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-18"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`on`](#on-7)

<a id="translate-18"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(`_`: (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`): `this`

Sets text to be shown positioned absolute on top of the visualization in the bottom-right corner. This is most often used in Geomaps to display the copyright of map tiles. The text is rendered as HTML, so any valid HTML string will render as expected (eg. anchor links work).

Defines the main color to be used for each data point in a visualization. Can be either an accessor function or a string key to reference in each data point. If a color value is returned, it will be used as is. If a string is returned, a unique color will be assigned based on the string.

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

`boolean` \| (() => `boolean`) \| [`Tree`](#tree)

##### colorScalePosition()

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-37"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Tree`](#tree)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Tree`](#tree)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="separation"></a>

##### separation()

> **separation**(`_`: `any`): `any`

Defined in: [charts/Tree.ts:312](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Tree.ts#L312)

The separation function between neighboring nodes.

From the [d3-hierarchy documentation](https://github.com/d3/d3-hierarchy#tree_separation):
> The separation accessor is used to separate neighboring nodes. The separation function is passed two nodes a and b, and must return the desired separation. The nodes are typically siblings, though the nodes may be more distantly related if the layout decides to place such nodes adjacent.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
function separation(a, b) {
return a.parent === b.parent ? 1 : 2;
}
```

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. <a id="treemap"></a>

### Treemap

Defined in: [charts/Treemap.ts:31](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Treemap.ts#L31)

Uses the [d3 treemap layout](https://github.com/mbostock/d3/wiki/Treemap-Layout) to creates SVG rectangles based on an array of data. See [this example](https://d3plus.org/examples/d3plus-hierarchy/getting-started/) for help getting started using the treemap generator.

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-38"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): `DataPoint`[] \| [`Treemap`](#treemap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

`DataPoint`[] \| [`Treemap`](#treemap)

###### Inherited from

[`Viz`](#viz).[`data`](#data-39)

<a id="detectresize-19"></a>

##### detectResize()

> **detectResize**(`_?`: `boolean`): `boolean` \| [`Treemap`](#treemap)

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1164](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1164)

If the width and/or height of a Viz is not user-defined, it is determined by the size of it's parent element. When this method is set to `true`, the Viz will listen for the `window.onresize` event and adjust it's dimensions accordingly.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |

##### shape()

Changes the primary shape used to represent each data point in a visualization. Not all visualizations support changing shapes, this method can be provided the String name of a D3plus shape class (for example, "Rect" or "Circle"), or an accessor Function that returns the String class name to be used for each individual data point.

| ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| ((`d`: `DataPoint`, `i`: `number`) => `string`) |

| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `any` |

###### Returns

`any`

###### Example

```ts
function comparator(a, b) {
return b.value - a.value;
}
```

Tells the subtitle whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the subtitle appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="viz"></a>

### Viz

Tells the colorScale whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the colorScale appears centered above the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

Defines which side of the visualization to anchor the color scale. Acceptable values are `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"`, and `false`. A `false` value will cause the color scale to not be displayed, but will still color shapes based on the scale.

<a id="data-39"></a>

##### data()

> **data**(`_?`: `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \}, `f?`: (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[]): [`Viz`](#viz) \| `DataPoint`[]

Defined in: [charts/Viz.ts:1125](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/charts/Viz.ts#L1125)

The primary data array used to draw the visualization. The value passed should be an *Array* of objects or a *String* representing a filepath or URL to be loaded. The following filetypes are supported: `csv`, `tsv`, `txt`, and `json`.

If your data URL needs specific headers to be set, an Object with "url" and "headers" keys may also be passed.

Additionally, a custom formatting function can be passed as a second argument to this method. This custom function will be passed the data that has been loaded, as long as there are no errors. This function should return the final array of obejcts to be used as the primary data array. For example, some JSON APIs return the headers split from the data values to save bandwidth. These would need be joined using a custom formatter.

If you would like to specify certain configuration options based on the yet-to-be-loaded data, you can also return a full `config` object from the data formatter (including the new `data` array as a key in the object).

Defaults to an empty array (`[]`).

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `_?` | `string` \| `DataPoint`[] \| \{ `headers`: `Record`\<`string`, `string`\>; `url`: `string`; \} | - |
| `f?` | (`data`: `DataPoint`[]) => `Record`\<`string`, `unknown`\> \| `DataPoint`[] | The data array or a URL string to load data from. |

###### Returns

[`Viz`](#viz) \| `DataPoint`[]

Tells the legend whether or not to use the internal padding defined by the visualization in it's positioning. For example, d3plus-plot will add padding on the left so that the legend appears centered underneath the x-axis. By default, this padding is only applied on screens larger than 600 pixels wide.

<a id="locale-19"></a>

##### locale()

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(): `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:156](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L156)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Returns

`string`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`locale`](#locale-7)

###### Call Signature

> **locale**(`_`: `string` \| `object`): `this`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:157](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L157)

The locale used for all text and number formatting. Supports the locales defined in [d3plus-format](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus-format/blob/master/src/locale.js). The locale can be a complex Object, a locale code (like "en-US"), or a 2-digit language code (like "en"). If a 2-digit code is provided, the "findLocale" function is used to identify the most approximate locale.

###### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `_` | `string` \| `object` |

###### Returns

`this`

###### Example

```ts
{
        separator: "",
        suffixes: ["y", "z", "a", "f", "p", "n", "\u00b5", "m", "", "k", "M", "B", "t", "q", "Q", "Z", "Y"],
        grouping: [3],
        delimiters: {
          thousands: ",",
          decimal: "."
        },
        currency: ["$", ""]
      }
```

<a id="on-19"></a>

##### on()

###### Call Signature

> **on**(): `Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:176](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L176)

Event listener for the specified event *typenames*. Mirrors the core [d3-selection](https://github.com/d3/d3-selection#selection_on) behavior.

###### Returns

`Record`\<`string`, (...`args`: `unknown`[]) => `unknown`\>

###### Example

```ts
new Plot
.on("click.Shape", function(d) {
console.log("data for shape clicked:", d);
})
.on("click.Legend", function(d) {
console.log("data for legend clicked:", d);
})
```

<a id="translate-19"></a>

##### translate()

###### Call Signature

> **translate**(): (`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

Defined in: [utils/BaseClass.ts:214](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/BaseClass.ts#L214)

Defines how informational text strings should be displayed. By default, this function will try to find the string in question (which is the first argument provided to this function) inside of an internally managed translation Object. If you'd like to override to use custom text, simply pass this method your own custom formatting function.

###### Returns

(`d`: `string`, `locale?`: `string`) => `string`

###### Example

```ts
.translate(function(d) {
return d === "Back" ? "Get outta here" : d;
})
```

###### Inherited from

[`BaseClass`](#baseclass).[`translate`](#translate-7)

## Functions

<a id="accessor"></a>

### accessor()

> **accessor**(`key`: `string`, `def?`: `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`): (`d`: `DataPoint`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`

Defined in: [utils/accessor.ts:14](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/accessor.ts#L14)

Wraps an object key in a simple accessor function.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `key` | `string` | The key to be returned from each Object passed to the function. |
| `def?` | `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint` | A default value to be returned if the key is not present. |

#### Returns

(`d`: `DataPoint`) => `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `DataPoint`

#### Examples

```ts
accessor("id");
```

```ts
function(d) {
return d["id"];
}
```

***

<a id="configprep"></a>

### configPrep()

> **configPrep**(`this`: `VizContext`, `config?`: `ConfigObject`, `type?`: `string`, `nest?`: `string` \| `false`): `ConfigObject`

Defined in: [utils/configPrep.ts:43](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/configPrep.ts#L43)

Preps a config object for d3plus data, and optionally bubbles up a specific nested type. When using this function, you must bind a d3plus class' `this` context.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `this` | `VizContext` | *required* | - |
| `config` | `ConfigObject` | `...` | The configuration object to parse. |
| `type` | `string` | `"shape"` | The event classifier to user for "on" events. For example, the default event type of "shape" will apply all events in the "on" config object with that key, like "click.shape" and "mouseleave.shape", in addition to any gloval events like "click" and "mouseleave". |
| `nest` | `string` \| `false` | `false` | An optional nested key to bubble up to the parent config level. |

#### Returns

`ConfigObject`

***

<a id="constant"></a>

### constant()

> **constant**\<`T`\>(`value`: `T`): () => `T`

Defined in: [utils/constant.ts:11](https://github.com/d3plus/d3plus/blob/b48c22667cace23541ff5baeea3f4f1dc9fd5e80/packages/core/src/utils/constant.ts#L11)

Wraps non-function variables in a simple return function.

#### Type Parameters

| Type Parameter |
| ------ |
| `T` |

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `value` | `T` | The value to wrap in a return function. |

#### Returns

() => `T`

#### Examples

```ts
constant(42);
```

```ts
function() {
return 42;
}
```
